root_text.an3.150:1.1: “Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an3.150:1.3: Akusalena kāyakammena, akusalena vacīkammena, akusalena manokammena—
root_text.an3.150:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an3.150:2.1: Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an3.150:2.3: Kusalena kāyakammena, kusalena vacīkammena, kusalena manokammena …pe….

translation_text.an3.150:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person has three qualities they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and reproved by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an3.150:1.3: Unskillful deeds by way of body, speech, and mind. …
translation_text.an3.150:2.1: When an astute, competent true person has three qualities they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an3.150:2.3: Skillful deeds by way of body, speech, and mind. …”
